commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer review of the scoped performance fix and any desired startup profiling proof.; Cleared: The current diff only threads an in-memory discovery result through plugin helper calls and does not add dependencies, CI changes, secret handling, downloaded code, or broader filesystem permissions. (sha=d6b8f468907c816bc5ee969d14ed7551a3f6f4df)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional PluginDiscoveryResult threading to bundled plugin runtime, bundled-source, and channel-catalog helpers, then reuses one discovery result across the scoped capability runtime retry loop.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes for the redundant discovery shape: current main source shows independent discoverOpenClawPlugins calls in the affected helpers and a retry loop that calls the bundled runtime loader twice. I did not run the TUI startup profiler in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer review of the scoped performance fix and any desired startup profiling proof.

target.title: perf(plugins): thread explicit discovery through capability runtime + retry loop
target.body:
## Summary

Multiple plugin subsystems independently invoke `discoverOpenClawPlugins` during the same startup flow, repeating identical filesystem walks. The retry loop in `loadScopedCapabilityRuntimeRegistryEntries` also re-discovers on each attempt.

This PR adds an optional `discovery?: PluginDiscoveryResult` parameter to:

- `loadBundledCapabilityRuntimeRegistry` (`src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.ts`)
- `resolveBundledPluginSources` (`src/plugins/bundled-sources.ts`)
- `listChannelCatalogEntries` (`src/plugins/channel-catalog-registry.ts`)

…and shares one discovery result across the two attempts in `loadScopedCapabilityRuntimeRegistryEntries` (`src/plugins/contracts/registry.ts`).

`discoverOpenClawPlugins` remains stateless. Sharing is function-scoped, matching the design constraint in `src/plugins/CLAUDE.md`:

> metadata stays fresh unless a caller owns an explicit `PluginMetadataSnapshot`, `PluginLookUpTable`, or manifest registry for the current flow. Do not add persistent metadata caches for discovery, manifest registries, installed-index reconstruction, owner lookup, model suppression, provider policy, public-artifact metadata, or similar control-plane answers.

Backward compatible: when `discovery` is not supplied, behavior is unchanged.

Closes #82308.

This commit reproduces @SebTardif's #75451 verbatim (with attribution) so we can land it on a fresher base and combine it with the profiling evidence below. Their PR can be closed in favor of this one.

## Context: what we measured

The freeze that motivated this change is real and severe. CPU profile (Node `--cpu-prof`, Bottom-Up view) of one TUI startup, with the process at `R+ 101% CPU` throughout:

| % self time | Activity |
|---|---|
| 24% | `lstat` (Node module resolution) |
| 13% | `open` |
| 13% (total) | `realpathSync` |
| 5% | `jiti` runtime TS transpile |
| 3% | `(garbage collector)` |
| 9.5% (total) | `tryReadJsonSync` (`dist/json-files-*.js`) |

Temporary `perfMark` instrumentation around `tryReadJsonSync` aggregated one startup run as:

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total sync JSON reads | **212,119** |
| Unique JSON paths read | **379** |
| Average reads per file | **~560×** |
| Plugin module loads | 248 (62 unique modules) |
| Loaded via `jiti` (slow path) | 119 (~48%) |

Top read offenders (one TUI run):

```
3838  ~/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/package.json
2166  ~/.openclaw/plugins/installs.json
1565  extensions/whatsapp/package.json
1565  extensions/anthropic-vertex/package.json
1565  extensions/amazon-bedrock/package.json
... (50+ extensions, all ~1565× each)
```

An event-loop heartbeat probe confirmed the freeze is synchronous JS, not I/O wait — `setInterval` ticks were 5–25 seconds late, never delivered during the freeze. Wall-clock duration was variable across runs (3.7s → 27s → 30s on the same hardware), tracking OS file cache temperature.

## What this PR does and doesn't fix

**Addressed:**
- Eliminates redundant outer discovery scans across the three subsystems above when callers share a `discovery` result.
- Eliminates the per-retry re-scan in `loadScopedCapabilityRuntimeRegistryEntries`.

**Not addressed (follow-ups):**
- `loader.ts` has two more `discoverOpenClawPlugins` calls (around lines 1693 and 2562) that could also accept threaded discovery.
- `installed-plugin-index-registry.ts`, `manifest-registry.ts`, and `config-contracts.ts` each call `discoverOpenClawPlugins` separately.
- The dominant cost — repeated **manifest reads** by Node's own module resolution during plugin module loading — is not addressed by discovery threading. With ~50 extensions read ~1500× each, the amplifier is mostly inside the loader / Node `require()` walks, not in outer discovery scans.
- ~48% of plugin loads still go through `jiti` (`tryNative: false`). Per the loader's own comment, that path adds "several seconds of per-load overhead on slower hosts." Separate problem.
- The `rolldown` rebuild that runs ahead of TUI start for source-checkout users (`scripts/run-node.mjs` → `scripts/tsdown-build.mjs`) is also a separate dev-workflow cost.

A conservative estimate is that this PR shaves a portion of the 25–30s freeze rather than eliminating it. Measurement after landing should tell us how much remains.

## Why this approach over a low-level cache

An earlier draft of this PR added a process-scoped memo at the `tryReadJsonSync` layer. That would have collapsed all 212K reads down to ~380 by caching each unique file's parsed value. It would have been far simpler and broader in coverage.

It was rejected (by reviewer commentary in #82308 and by `src/plugins/CLAUDE.md`'s explicit guidance) because:

- A persistent cache at a low layer hides staleness from every caller above it — including the gateway daemon's install/uninstall hot-reload paths.
- It violates the "single source of truth" property the rule is designed to preserve.
- Function-scoped threading makes the sharing visible at call sites, type-checked, and naturally bounded by the flow's lifetime.

This PR takes the architecturally aligned path. The cost is that the fix is narrower in coverage and likely needs follow-up PRs to thread `discovery?` through the remaining callers.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsgo --noEmit --project tsconfig.core.json` — clean
- [x] `npx oxfmt --check` and `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --quiet` on touched files — clean
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/bundled-sources.test.ts src/plugins/channel-catalog-registry.test.ts` — 14/14 pass
- [ ] End-to-end TUI startup before/after with perf instrumentation to quantify read-count drop (run with `OPENCLAW_PERF_HEARTBEAT=1` and per-call `perfMark` patches available from the audit branch; not gated on this PR)

Behavior addressed: TUI startup re-runs `discoverOpenClawPlugins` multiple times per launch when distinct plugin subsystems each invoke it without coordinating; the retry loop in `loadScopedCapabilityRuntimeRegistryEntries` re-discovers on each attempt.
Real environment tested: macOS, Node 22.22, source checkout against a separate `pnpm gateway:watch` gateway. Same setup that produced the read counts above with temporary instrumentation.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/bundled-sources.test.ts src/plugins/channel-catalog-registry.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo --noEmit --project tsconfig.core.json`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`; `npx oxfmt --check`.
Evidence after fix: lint/format/typecheck clean; the three changed-module test suites pass (14/14); the added `discovery?` param is optional so all existing callers are unaffected.
Observed result after fix: SebTardif's original PR (#75451) verified the function-scoped sharing pattern type-checks and tests pass. End-to-end startup deltas should be measured by reviewers running the TUI against a real gateway with this patch applied.
What was not tested: end-to-end TUI startup timing on this branch; the broader extension to `loader.ts`, `installed-plugin-index-registry.ts`, `manifest-registry.ts`, `config-contracts.ts` is left for follow-up.
target.author: romneyda
